questions.question: Why was the child excited at the beginning of the story?
questions.answer: Because he was going to the fair with his parents.

questions.question: Why did the parents keep calling the child?
questions.answer: Because he lagged behind while observing things on the way.

questions.question: Why did the child not insist on buying the toy?
questions.answer: Because he knew his father would refuse.

questions.question: Why did the child want burfi from the sweet shop?
questions.answer: Because burfi was his favourite sweet.

questions.question: Why did the child move on without waiting for an answer from his parents?
questions.answer: Because he knew they would refuse his request.

questions.question: Why did the child want the garland of flowers?
questions.answer: Because he was attracted by the colourful flowers.

questions.question: Why did the child desire the balloons?
questions.answer: Because of their bright rainbow colours.

questions.question: Why did the child not stop near the snake-charmer?
questions.answer: Because his parents had forbidden him from listening to such music.

questions.question: Why did the child request a ride on the roundabout?
questions.answer: Because he saw others enjoying the ride.

questions.question: What made the child realise he was lost?
questions.answer: He could not see his parents around him.

questions.question: How did the child react after realizing he was lost?
questions.answer: He cried loudly and searched for his parents.

questions.question: Why did the child run towards the temple?
questions.answer: Because he thought he might find his parents there.

questions.question: Why did the man pick up the child?
questions.answer: Because he heard the child crying.

questions.question: Why did the man offer the child different attractions?
questions.answer: To make him happy and stop his crying.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse the roundabout ride?
questions.answer: Because he wanted his parents.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse the balloons offered by the man?
questions.answer: Because he missed his parents.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse the garland?
questions.answer: Because he only wanted his mother and father.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse sweets?
questions.answer: Because he was more worried about finding his parents.

questions.question: What does the child’s behaviour show after getting lost?
questions.answer: It shows his strong love for his parents.

questions.question: What does the crowded fair symbolize in the story?
questions.answer: Confusion and danger for a lost child.

questions.question: What lesson does the child learn in the story?
questions.answer: That parents are more important than material things.

questions.question: What emotion dominates the story after the child gets lost?
questions.answer: Fear.

questions.question: What does the child’s repeated cry show?
questions.answer: His desperation to find his parents.

questions.question: What quality does the stranger show?
questions.answer: Kindness and sympathy.

questions.question: What is the central message of the story?
questions.answer: Family love is more valuable than material pleasures.
